message.content:
For apartment maintenance and occasional DIY work, you generally want tools that are:

• Reliable  
• Affordable  
• Not overly heavy-duty  
• Widely available  
• Compatible within one battery system (if going cordless)

Practical, good‑value brands for this level of use:

• Ryobi – One of the best choices for apartment DIY. Huge 18V tool ecosystem, good quality for the price, and very accessible for beginners.  
• Craftsman – Solid mid‑range option, decent performance, and reasonably priced.  
• Black+Decker – Budget‑friendly and perfectly fine for light home tasks.  
• Skill/Skil – Good quality at an affordable price, especially their cordless tools.  

If you want something a bit more premium but still not “contractor expensive”:

• Makita (CXT line) – Lighter and compact, great for indoor/apartment work.  
• DeWalt – High quality, rugged, and long‑lasting, though usually more expensive than you need for occasional DIY.

Typical setup for an apartment:

• Cordless drill/driver  
• Small impact driver (optional but helpful)  
• Multipurpose hand tools (screwdrivers, pliers, utility knife)  
• Stud finder  
• Measuring tape  
• Compact tool bag  

Best all‑around pick for most people: Ryobi 18V One+ — strong value, easy to use, and tools for almost anything you’ll ever do in an apartment.

If you want, I can suggest a starter kit based on your budget.
